You can tell that he greatly values time– he always starts and ends class exactly as listed and lets you go early as long as all the content was covered. One thing to note is the structure of free lectures– if you don't have any specific questions to ask, don't bother going (actually, he encourages you not to come to free lectures/office hours if you don't have a clear goal). Also as a heads up, the class average does get rounded down to a B depending on the section so it's possible that you may experience a down curve by the end of the quarter.

Prof Huang is an amazing lecturer who tells you exactly what you need to know for the exams. He comes prepared to teach and explain difficult/confusing concepts clearly. TAs grade too harshly on exams.

If you took high school physics and did well, this class will probably be relatively easy.

The midterms are only three questions and the final was six questions, so small errors can affect your grade significantly.

The "projects" took around eight hours each but get graded really lightly. The lower quartile on them was still 100%. They are good experience for statics, MatLab, and writing reports and 5% of your class grade each though, so you should probably take them somewhat seriously.

There were significant grading errors during the second midterm, I ended up getting 19% back when I went office hours.
hard content
Necessary course for every McCormick student.
I felt like the TAs and Professor Huang were very helpful and explained things very clearly and logically, especially if you go to the free lectures and office hours.
If you have taken prior physics courses, much of this course is just a review/extension of statics.
The curve in this class sucks. Every class is curved exactly to a B. For example, our first midterm median was like 96%, making the curve one of the worst I have ever seen.

I think this course was chill. If you took AP physics (mechanics) in high school I feel like you'll have a good foundation. A lot of the class is formulaic, and the problems general all follow the same procedure to solve. Start the design projects early, they do take a long time.
Overall instruction is good as long as you go to lecture.

If you took physics in highschool and already took math 228–1, this class is going to be a breeze. I literally never went to discussion (which means I didn't go to anything for the last two weeks) and I got an A with relative ease. Midterm 1 was incredibly easy, Midterm 2 was relatively hard, and the final was right in the middle. For the design projects, don't save it for the night before. The problems are easy but the report takes a long time to make.
I wanted to learn more systems, it got repetitive and boring quite quickly
It's an ok class. Bit difficult if you have never worked with vectors before, a breeze if you have.

Huang is only present for lectures. Lecture days are packed with information, and he only teaches you the content for a simple example, and it's your job to learn yourself how to apply it to different and more complicated problems.
This class isn't very hard but requires a lot of effort since its curved down to a 3.0. First midterm had a 92 average but that was curved to a B. Not great.
The material isn't very hard, just don't be like me and get over confident. You can perform well with adequate studying, although a good background a physics also helps.

EA II was a lot less confusing than EA I. However, the exams are high stress and high stakes. There are only three questions, and many people get 100% which does not help considering the curve is harsh. Though I enjoyed the content and lectures, my grades suffered despite studying and attending office hours regularly. Professor Huang is an amazing lecturer and easy to understand. The TAs were also great–definitely attend!
Thought statics and forces were cool. Material is way more tangible and applicable than the abstract linear algebra you learn in EA1 so I did a lot better in this class. Huang is a superhuman.

I do not understand why anyone thinks this class is something every engineer should take. I barely learned everything over the entire quarter, and the content that was covered was taught within 7 weeks. As a chemical engineer, I find it hard to believe any of these ideas will be relevant ot my future, and I think anyone would be much better off taking a physics: mechanics class over this. It makes little sense to me that there is a physics course being taught to engineers that does not assume knowledge of calculus. Overall, not sure what I learned, but I do not this class freaked a lot of people out and even got them to drop engineering, so I am really not sure who this class is meant to help. Regardless, the actual teaching is excellent. Huang is a fantastic professor and does an excellent job of teaching the limited content covered in the class.

Taking htis class with Huang was very simple. He explained the topics very clearly in class with multiple examples and then we'd practice them in recitation. The exams varied sometimes from the material on the practice midterms. The content was very similar to high school physics or a statics class.

The course is quite different from EA1, but I think it is easier since it does not involve a lot of Matlab. Prof Huang is a very good option to take it with since he teaches a lot of what will be in the exams. His teaching style is very nice, as he goes over only what is necessary.

At the beginning, it didn't really make sense to me, but it made more sense as the course went on. Huang was a very thorough teacher whose lecture made the concepts understandable, and his tests weren't that bad.

I thought this was a fair class. The instruction was great, and the discussions helped me get an idea of what to expect on the exams. While the class is curved to a B, I think it wasn't hard to do well as long as you do all the practice problems provided.
Good

Professor Huang is a great professor! Would recommend taking it with him! He's a clear lecturer, he can be funny, and he provides you with all the resources needed to succeed. It's definitely one of the easier courses in the EA sequence so far, a lot easier than EA 1, and oftentimes the professor is done with the material two weeks early, so you essentially have a couple weeks where you don't have classes. The tests are all not too hard either, so I definitely enjoyed this course!

This course is a pretty simple statics class and professor Huang was a good instructor. If tests are easier there will however be grade deflation as each class section is curved to a B so you’re competing with your peers

Professor Huang focused on applications of concepts by doing example problems mostly directly from the book. The course wasn't that interesting to me as the concepts were not intellectually challenging and the material was mainly relevant to civil engineering while I'm a computer Engineering major.
I honestly really appreciated the homework I felt like it really helped engrain the material in

Huang is a quick professor. Explains concepts well and has "free" lectures the week before reading week because he goes through content so quickly. He doesn't teach springs so spring forces aren't on the midterm.

I thought the homework questions were a great way to learn material but I did not like how it took so much time each week for only 10% of your grade. The design projects also took so long and it's only 10% of your grade. If they were 15% and 15% then the final would still be worth 35% which is a higher percentage than the other introduction classes's final which are 30%.
Haung's section tends to do better. I would recommend take the class with Flemming and go to Huang's class this way the exam is easier and the curve will be nicer.

This course just overviews general physics concepts. If you have taken AP Physics BC you’ll be fine. If you have taken any Physics really, you’ll be fine. Huang writes the final exam, so you’ll be used to the type of questions asked if you take this class with him.

Reasonable course, have to take for engineering degree. Felt like I did not actually learn a lot in the class, as the same 3 concepts were just repeated throughout the quarter in different contexts. Take it with Huang if you can

I really liked the professor and the class was helpful. Some people said he moved too slowly but as someone who went to a very bad highschool with no physics program, it was very helpful. Highly recommend taking it w Huang

This course moved at a good pace and I feel that the mixture of recitations and lectures really allowed me to learn the material. The test difficulty was somewhat inconsistent and did not always reflect was what taught in the course. Also, rounding made it somewhat difficult to get a good grade as means were very dependent on who was in your class.

This isn't a fun class. The grading system sucks, the teachers don't teach the material very well, and the tests are inconsistent. Just keep in mind that everybody has to take this class, and are suffering as much as you. Put your head down, do the work, and get through it. One thing I can say is the material is much easier to understand than EA1, especially if you took Physics in HS.
I think the course was good in allowing me to understand how to break down very large technical problems.

EA 2 is a required class, and Professor Huang is not a bad professor to take it with. He is a fine lecturer once you adjust to his prescriptive lecturing style. He is not amazing at explaining *why* something makes sense conceptually, but if you listen to him and do as he says, you will do fine in the class. Though, in the end I do not feel like I have a good understanding of *why* we did what we did. Why do we use moment? Why do I need to know it? How is it used in real life? Professor Huang's conceptual explanations were sometimes lacking, in favor of prescriptive explanations of how to crunch numbers.

This being said, when I asked questions of him after class, he always really made sure I understood his answer and that I did not leave confused, which was nice.

It is commonly said that Professor Huang's exams are harder than those of the other sections, and I found this to be marginally true. Despite this fact, I found that the more difficult midterms helped me prepare for the final better.

The weekly problem sets were relatively involved, but nothing out of the ordinary.

EA 2 is a class many people prefer to EA 1, but I found myself leaning the opposite direction. EA 2 is also more physics and civil engineering as opposed to EA 1's focus on linear algebra and matlab.

Overall, I felt nothing particularly strong about the class aside from grades being curved down to a B. The method and mechanical of curving were not well explained during the class and it wasn't clear if the curve was section by section, grade by grade, exam by exam, or whether the entire grade was curved at the end.

Huang is a solid teacher who organizes his class in a great way. Most of the time is spent doing example problems as the content isn't that difficult. Class can be hard though because its all about limiting small mistakes.

I think that this class provides you all the material you need to learn, but it is essential you go to lectures. There aren't really materials provided if you miss a lecture, and this is the primary way of learning any new material. The homework is reasonable, but should be worked on throughout the week.
Prof Huang is a great professor, so I would recommend taking it with him. The homework is pretty straightforward and the tests aren't bad at all.
HUANG IS THE GOAT take it with him you won't regret it Content is easy enough, Huang makes it fun

EA 2 is a difficult course, even if you've taken physics before. I had taken mechanics, but this was pretty different since it's statics and focuses on engineering applications. The first midterm wasn't too difficult, but the average was 91, which was curved to a B! The second midterm was more difficult and the average was 71, which was also curved to a B. The two design projects were full of painful busy work and took at least 8 hours. Prof. Huang was a good teacher, but he doesn't post lecture notes or recordings (I don't think any professors do though, to be fair).

EA2 is pretty easy. Just do the homeworks (which are pulled straight from the textbook) and read the relevant chapters in the textbook. I think attending lecture is pretty useless since the material is not challenging.

I wouldn't say that EA 2 is a particularly engaging class because the content is pretty easy. However, the course is curved to a B average, whether that be up or down. Professor Huang was an excellent teacher who made everything feel very straightforward.

Take it with Prof. Huang. His lectures are easy to follow, and he teaches very efficiently––we end 2 weeks earlier than other sessions. EA2 is in my opinion a lot easier than EA1, as long as you go to class and pay attention, you're fine.
This course has about 1/2 the lectures as free lecures (office hours for huang) because there is in fact very little content they get through
NA
I really enjoyed EA2 and found it to be highly valuable. What stood out the most to me was how the course made complex concepts in physics and math more understandable.

This is the course for mechanical engineers – it's quite interesting sitting in the EA sequence. I enjoyed the logic behind the course – I believe the way we solve problems in EA2 is important for any engineer to understand.

Honestly a pretty chill course. The material is straightforward and you do not really need to read the textbook if you have Huang. He teaches you everything you need to know with in class examples and everything is structured neatly. The midterms will be hard and have a relatively low average but each class is curved to a B.

source_file: Student Report for Renee Engeln (PSYCH_110-0_20  Introduction to Psychology)_5e7b85d4-00ee-4563-903d-867cab3c4008en-US.pdf

likert_questions.question_text: Provide an overall rating of the instruction.
Provide an overall rating of the course.
Estimate how much you learned in the course.
Rate the effectiveness of the course in challenging you intellectually.
Rate the effectiveness of the instructor in stimulating your interest in the subject.

categorical_questions.question_text: Estimate the average number of hours per week you spent on this course outside of class and lab time
What is the name of your school?
Your Class
What is your reason for taking the course?
What was your interest in this subject before taking the course?

comments: Take this course with professor Renee, she's probably the best lecturer I've had here in my time at Northwestern. Super funny lectures, loves seeing her students succeed, very warm and friendly. As for the course, content gets more interesting once you're past the biological component (structure of the eye, etc). DO NOT take this course if you're expecting a free A, you still have to put in time and effort to study for exams, but professor renee gives a lot of extra credit support to help you achieve the grade you want.

i was originally really excited for this course, but after finding out that literally 3/4 of this course is just self taught i was really disappointed and became really uninterested. getting an a is definitely possible as its all multiple choice, however you need to put in adequate work into mastering the unlogical way some of the questions are asked.

I really liked this course!! Professor Engeln has super interesting and funny lectures. There are many opportunities for extra credit both inside and outside class. The concepts aren't too difficult, but there are a lot of them. That being said, exams are fairly easy if you study (quizlet!!), especially since she gives a list of review questions with the topics that will be on the exam. The class is super interesting, and if you pace yourself well, it is pretty easy.

This class is great! Professor Engeln is really engaging in lecture and also gives great tips for studying. As long as you listen to her advice, you should be fine. There are three non–cumulative exams which really breaks up the content (thankfully, because there is a lot) and makes it more manageable. Lots of opportunities for in–class and extra credit which boost your grade.

This course was really fun and pretty easy to do well in. It can be easy to put off, leaving you with a lot of work to catch up on, but none of it is very difficult. Definitely recommend taking at some point, even if you just want to learn some cool things.

This class is the best!! Renee is an engaging lecturer. There is a huge range of content covered in this course, and the grade is mostly based on three non–cumulative exams. We did not get into social psychology, personality, and disorders until the end of week 7. The exams are challenging because a broad range of information is covered, but an A/A– is very doable, especially with ample extra credit.

This course was very interesting, I always loved psychology and the professor made it even more appealing. There was a lot of content, I felt that it was way too much for an intro course because the exams covered so much content and it felt too fast paced. The content itself way very clear and straightforward, it was difficult to study for all the content.
I loved this course. It was the right pace and was not overwhelming. The assessments were also really fair.

This is probably one of the best classes I've taken at NU so far. I'm not gonna sugarcoat it, the tests are hard, assignments are long. But wow. Renee is such an amazing lecturer, and although it was an hour twenty minute lecture, I always found it extremely engaging and rewarding. The concepts are really interesting, and she just has such a compelling way of lecturing.
The course is very fun and engaging without being too much work or too difficult.

Take this class!! You will learn so much about yourself and the world around you and Professor Engeln does a great job of making every lecture very engaging with real–life and relatable examples. The class consists of 3 exams and many chances for extra credit opportunities, so as long as you do homeworks you should be fine!

Most interesting course I've taken so far. If you're considering diving into the world of psychology, this intro class with Professor Engeln is a fantastic starting point. You'll get a taste of a variety of topics, enough to know what really grabs your interest without getting bogged down in too much detail. What sets this class apart is Professor Engeln’s way of teaching – she is really passionate, she really loves what she teaches and she breaks down ideas into something you can really grasp, moving beyond just throwing definitions at you. It’s this approach that not only made the subject matter click for me but also made the class genuinely enjoyable. Plus, the exams are relatively easy (all multiple choice) compared to most intro classes, and there's not much work required outside of class. I do recommend attending the classes, though, as many exam questions come from discussions or even offhand remarks made during lectures. The only downside? You're required to participate in multiple studies, which is a mixed bag—some are fascinating, but others can be pretty tedious. Despite that, I'd definitely recommend this course to anyone looking to explore psychology in an engaging and low–pressure environment.

Renee will challenge you. You can take this course as an easy distro with other professors. But her class will teach you how to study and how to do it effectively. It's a hurdle but one that will teach you how to overcome any future hurdle.

Renee is the best! She makes class super engaging and interesting. The tests can be challenging for an intro level psych course, but as long as you do all the practice questions you will know everything you need to for the exam. 3 non–cumulative tests that are done before reading week. Highly recommend!

I think I only started getting really interested in this class towards the end of the class. I really enjoyed learning about personality psych, but getting through the more structural/technical parts of Intro Psych was difficult and felt harder to grasp.

This class and Renee are great! A lot of people end up dropping this class because it's harder than people expect and midterm scores are a substantial component of the grade. A lot of people are taken off guard by the level of detail on the first midterm. I think the content is not necessarily hard, but not all of it is "common sense" or completely intuitive. The (all multiple choice) exams are not hard but require thoughtful preparation; if you study hard you will do well. I appreciated the little extra credit opportunities. Again, Engeln is fantastic and super engaging.
I came into this course knowing a good amount of psych but having never taken a psych class, and it definitely filled in the gaps of my knowledge.
The material is interesting, but the class requires studying.

I liked this class — Renee made lectures engaging, and it wasn't too much work, just a lot of memorization. I don't know if I'd take it if I could go back and choose another social science distro, but it was interesting to learn the basic foundations and history of psych.
Yes it very helpful in understanding how to relate concepts.
Prof. Engeln is a great professor, she is very entertaining and enthusiastic when lecturing and the material presented in class and the readings is what is on the exams
The class was really interesting and the instructor really helped to keep everyone engaged in the lecture.
This course was fantastic. It teaches basic psychological concepts that every person should know and the teacher was a phenomenal lecturer.
Not an easy A but k really enjoyed the class with Renee. She’s an awesome professor the coruse is just extremely memorization heavy and the exams are a lot of the grade.
This class is great! Renee is super interesting and the lectures are involved without being too much work out of class.

It was definitely a good intro to psych class– it covered a big range of topics but lowkey it was kinda boring. I think the instruction was very like mid not that it was bad just that it was the same thing every class.

This has easily been my favorite course I've taken at Northwestern so far. Professor Renee was such an engaging lecturer and made it exciting to come to every class. There was so much material covered, but it wasn't to difficult to pick it up by staying on top of the Inquizitives and looking at the questions posted for the textbook and lectures. I feel like I learned so much, and I'm have more of a passion for psychology than when I started this class. The tests weren't too hard, especially because you're told the exact topics you need to be familiar with, and they're all multiple choice with few tricky questions as long as you feel comfortable with the content. If you don't wait until the last minute to do the review questions or Inquizitives, and you really pay attention in class, the workload for this is extremely manageable and really only took an hour or two outside of class each week. I'd highly recommend anyone who might be interested in psychology to take this course.
The lectures were very interesting and the midterms were very sustainable.

super interesting class!! renee is the best— lectures were so engaging and entertaining. tests are fair but it does take a lot of studying to memorize everything. however, she gave us several extra credit opportunities and the test averages were pretty good so i think it's easy to get a decent grade without stressing too much. the inquizitives are so annoying though

Has quite a few moving components, from the exams, the online homework (InQuizitives, which were more frustrating than informative) and the psych study participation. I think everything is generally low intensity, but can be a reasonable amount of work as it adds up.
Professor Engeln was very enthusiastic and explained concepts well in psychology. The exams were manageable with sufficient studying, and it was a good distro to take.

I took this class with Professor Engeln, and I loved it! Im going to be honest, this class will start off quite intimidating with the biological part and the more conceptual parts of psych, but honestly use the REVIEW questions given by her and the LECTURE questions and you will do amazing on the exams! not only that, but this is such an engaging class, every class period had something funny happen during it, making me want to keep coming to class. MAKE SURE YOU GO TO CLASS! I dont know how many times I need to extend this! there are so many in class extra credit oppurtunities so take advantage of that!
I had a relatively enjoyable time in the class. It definitely can be challenging, so pay attention to lectures from the beginning. Highly recommend.

Take this course with Renee!! Super engaging lectures and very well–taught. Not an "easy" A but an achievable A –– you can always be fully prepared for an exam if you use the review questions she gives. Her class is a little more biology/anatomy focused, but overall a great course to take whether or not you are interested in Psych.

The class is basically no work and very manageable. I did not do too well on exams but exams are completely manageable. Honestly I think it depends on how much you are interested in the content. I was not interested very much despite having a really good teacher.

professor Engeln was amazing, the course was not difficult if you read the textbook and took notes during class and did the inquizatives. the exams were fair and she is such an engaging professor. you will me laughing most of the classes! easy A if you put in the work and are good at memorizing alot of information

Take this class with Renee if you can!! She's such an entertaining lecturer and makes the class very engaging. Although classes were not recorded, it was not too hard to keep up with the pace of each lecture. A lot of the class is very memorization–heavy and requires you to memorize extremely minute details of experiments that are not very significant as well as many terms. Your grade is also nearly completely dependent on exams (86%), so it's important to properly prepare for them by going through the textbook and the notes that you take in class.
I thought that the course did a good job as an introductory course, but there were definitely a few instances where we as students would question whether we were even learning psychology or anatomy.

It was so much information in such a short amount of time. It being a lecture class made it super difficult to focus and learn, especially because we didn't cover all the test material in class. Renee is great but it was really hard. The lab requirements didn't take as long as I thought they would but they were annoying.
The professor was good at stirring interest in students. The material was conveyed well, and the class was interactive when it needed to be.
This course is fun! There is a lot of memorization but if you pace it out it is pretty manageable.

The classes were generally engaging, and the fact that the professor always tried to bring the topics to our reality made it easier to memorize and understand concepts. Psych is a vast field, so it can be a lot of information to take, especially when it gets closer to exams, but if you have good study strategies, you will be fine!
Great teacher

Readings kind of annoying and long but just do them because they pair well with the lectures and help reinforce the learning. Tests all MCQ which is alright but gotta spread out studying because craving is too hard for this class
I learned a lot about myself and the people around me

This was a great course. The tests were difficult, but fair. The proffessor is an incredible lecturer who makes all classes incredibly interesting, which is hard to do for an hour and a hlaf long class! I look forward to taking more classes with her in the future.
This course is assessed very fairly. There are lots of opportunities to improve your grade outside of exams.
Extremely well organized and interesting. I learned a lot

The course is structured for students to succeed. Renee is a excellent, engaging lecturer and make the 80 minute class far more manageable. The readings are a bit long, but are reasonably interesting and the questions for each reading can be completed without having actually done the reading. There are review questions that you can study almost exclusively for each exams which are then extremely reasonable if have studied the questions. The exams are also all multiple choice, however they do occur every 2 weeks so it does feel like you are constantly studying and taking midterms. There are also lots of opportunities for extra credit.
Just study the review questions, and also understand that the questions are worded a little weirdly sometimes. Really read the question, and you'll be fine!
I really liked that this class allowed me to apply the concepts to the real world.
I loved the lectures and lecturer. Straightforward exams and the professor would give us review questions that basically dictated what would be on the exam. Made me love psych even more.
I liked coming to this class simply because the lectures were so enjoyable and the content and work along with it was always set up perfectly to coincide.

The professor is one of the best I have ever taken. She is funny and keeps you engaged throughout the lecture. I liked the content of course and just do the review questions and inquizitves and you will be fine. I don't think any class should put this much weight on exams though, she does have a lot of extra credit but i think the grades should be distributed differently. I wish the lectures followed the readings more. But still great class!! Easy if you study for the exams.
Renee is a really good teacher who is very passionate about her work. The class was always very engaging and interesting. However, this is definitely not an easy A if that is your idea coming in.

Professor Engeln is really passionate at teaching Psychology and she tries to make the class engaging for every student. However, the class has a larger workload than I imagined with the 2 chapters of reading per week and the 12 mandatory psych labs. It also involves a large amount of memorization for every test.

Kinda boring class, but Engeln is a great lecturer, but she only covers half of what is actually covered on the test, so half of the content is just from the textbook. The curriculum is very similar to AP Psych
I loved this class, it was very effective in learning so much information in such a short period of time.
I was very happy with this course and learned way more than I ever expected.

The material is not hard and if you pace correctly is extremely doable, show up to lecture because there are in class assignments that count towards your grade. Do the extra credit because they are pretty easy points.

This class is not an easy A. The exams are so hard and honestly quite unfair. The rest of the course is manageable but the exams are impossible due to questions from lecture that were mentioned for no more than 30 seconds and lectures are not recorded. Renee is a quite beloved professor but she yaps a lot and it is hard to distinguish the important material from her tangents. Think hard before taking this course.

I thought that the instruction was okay. Renee is a very engaging and clearly very intelligent professor, but I find that she leaves a lot of the complicated concepts to the homework/reading and explains the more intuitive stuff in class.

I thought this class would be a relatively easy one, but I was wrong. The amount of reading that the professor assigns is a ton –– I found this being my most time–consuming course. Lectures are fun and course material is engaging, but the reading is just overpowering. Something like 500 pages.

I think Psych is very interesting and this is a pretty standard Intro to Psych course. The content is mostly learned through the textbook, so you definitely need to do the readings and it helped to have taken good notes. The tests were 80–90% content from the textbook, and the rest were random questions from lectures. Lectures mostly went over the textbook content

This was a really fun and informative class. Renee is such a fun and passionate lecturer and she made me want to learn. There are 3 non–cumulative tests and you are given review questions detailing exactly what you need to study for and what will be on the test. Overall, this was just a very enjoyable class.

I have very mixed feelings about this course. The lecture was not really what I expected and I feel like Renee is a little disorganized. While she was always on time and what was expected of each student was clear, I didn't feel like her teaching always came across in a clear sense and I feel like she sometimes enjoyed telling stories and making jokes on stage over actually teaching. I also felt the lectures themselves weren’t the most engaging and that the slides could have been presented better visually, and like the information could have been organized more coherently with more writing/guidance on each slide. She does post chapter and lecture review questions for each lecture that are helpful in preparing for each exam, but she doesn’t post the answers so you have to go to office hours or ask a friend if there’s something you don’t know. The first two units (and subsequent tests) are honestly not very interesting and the class only really began being enjoyable during the third section. 86% of your grade is based on three multiple choice exams, which were not easy. The first two were hard and I know several people (myself included) who were struggling in the class and had done very poorly. The other part of your grade comes from inquizitives, in class extra credit, and then extra credit papers. Overall, if you are a good test taker, especially multiple choice, take this class. I didn’t do nearly as well as I would have liked to in this class and I kind of regret taking it, because I think it unnecessarily (this wasn’t for any distro or major requirement) brought down my GPA.
I loved its neuroscince aspect

Great class. Renee is a great professor. If you go to every class and take notes and you study her review questions before the exam, you will do well. The review questions prepare you very well for each exam.

I really liked this class and loved Professor Engeln. Her lectures are very engaging and she is clearly passionate about teaching. Everything you need to study for the exams is in the review questions she posts. Do the review questions after each lecture (if you can) and stay up to date with readings and inquizitives! Do not leave all review Q's until right before the exam just to make it easier for yourself. I made a quizlet of all the review Q's and necessary terms and just repeated the flashcards over and over until I knew everything –– I think putting effort into your answers for the review Q's and flashcards are the best way to do well in this class. Go to office hours! She is super helpful. Definitely recommend.

I loved professor Englen, this was definitely one of my favorite classes I have taken, especially for a big lecture class like this. Lecture was very engaging, and I really appreciated the way she made sure we understood concepts. You do have to do the readings and go to lecture, but lecture is interesting and the review questions she gives are pretty accurate to what the exam covers.
The professor was very engaging and made lectures fun. Tests were all multiple choice, and readings were manageable.
I found this course very interesting, and the material wasn't hard to grasp.

TAKE THIS WITH RENEE. She is extremely knowledgable about the subject and an incredibly entertaining and smart lecturer. Loved going to her class. Exams are not easy, follow her tips on how to study: do the review questions, do the inquizzitives and reading for the week DURING THE WEEK, and dont leave studying until the last moment. I learned a lot and I believe everyone should take this class regardless of their major.

Professor E is a great professor. This class is engaging and a good introductory class to psych; even though I'd never taken psych in high school, keeping up with the content was not unmanageable. Exams are difficult in that they're all application–based questions, but not incredibly awful. Do the review questions she gives! She gives out tools to try to help people succeed in this class.

This course taught me many new things and Professor Engeln made the class very engaging. The exams are a little hard but if you keep up with the material and do the review questions you should be fine. Overall great class.

Renee is a good professor since she always answers all your questions and tries to add relevant examples to the modern–day world. I did find it a little difficult to pay attention during the whole class period because she would sometimes talk about a subject for too long and I lost focus on what the whole point was.
This class is awesome & so much fun. Renee is the best lecturer I have ever had at NU

prof. engeln does a really great job at keeping lectures digestible, engaging, and interesting. there are three exams that consist entirely of multiple choice. exams are relatively difficult and require a lot of memorization, but if you give yourself enough time to study, you should be fine.

This class was really interesting and also really challenging. This class will force you to perfect your study skills. but you will also walk away with a lot more knowledge than you had before. The class isn't easy, it ended up being one of my biggest stressors this quarter, but it was worthwhile. Don't take this class if you're looking for something easy, but if you have an interest in psychology then it's totally worth it. Also, Renee was a GREAT teacher, she made the class super engaging.
It was a great course overall! Highly recommend.
Great intro to psych class! Super interesting and fun, you will do well in this class as long as you go to lectures.
If you put in the work for this class you will be rewarded. You just have to put in the work. Luckily professor Engeln does an amazing job at giving review materials and extra credit.
It was a super fun and educational course! I love that I could apply everything I learned to daily life.

Take Psych 110 with Renee! She's so engaging and entertaining and I was always excited to come to class every week. The exams are not bad at all and as long as you study you'll be fine. The only unit I didn't really like was the neuro unit because I'm not a fan of biology, but other than that I loved learning about concepts that can be applied to my daily life. This course is what has inspired me to do a Psych minor because I enjoyed it so much.
models due after exam. three exams prof give study guides.

I took this class as a theme requirement and it felt a lot like high school classes. Renee is an amazing lecturer and provides so many materials to help students. 3 non cumulative exams were mainly just memorization
Great introductory psychology course. Definitely do the textbook readings as the exams go over things that were not necessarily discussed in class. Great prep for the psych/soc section of the MCAT
Fair course. As long as you do the lecture questions/textbook questions that Renee posts you'll be fine for the tests.
Very interesting and engaging class. You get what you put into it. If you took AP Psych in high school some of the materials might be familiar to you

This course was not too bad. It's a lot of memorization but what can you expect from Intro to Psych. I recommend taking this course if you are interested in Psych or it's a great distro it's very interesting and you learn a lot. However, Professor EngeIn's lectures are mostly just her talking rather than going thru the concepts. I wish she would go over the terms more in depth. The work is easy and self explanatory. Just have to study a lot for the midterms.

I loved this course!!! Definitely take it with Prof. Engeln if you can, she is the best. Super interesting material, the tests are not cumulative, and this class was often the best part of my week especially during a tough winter quarter. Also, attend class!!!! There are a lot of extra credit opportunities during throughout the quarter, and the in–class assignments are an easy way to boost your grade.

I think this is a great course as an introduction to the topic of psychology. The homework assignments were easy and actually helped with learning the material. The exams are not too hard as long as you take good notes and study for a few hours.
The professor is a sweetheart but the class structure makes it hard to learn and/or care. Didn't find myself enamoured with any of the subject matter whatsoever.

This class was honestly a lot easier than I expected. As long as you go to lectures and do the readings, you will have no problem on the exams. There are also plenty of extra credit opportunities to boost your grade throughout the quarter. Renee was a great professor and I was glad I took this class.

Take this class with Renee! She makes lectures extremely entertaining, and I found myself constantly engaged. This course is very content–heavy and moves at a fast pace, but if you are able to keep up with the readings, Inquizitives, and attending each lecture, you'll be able to understand the content and do well on the 3 exams.
I learned a lot about how the brain works and how we interact. Great course.
This course is a fun, but very fast paced intro to psych. Would recommend to anyone thinking about studying psych or with a genuine interest in it. Renee is a good lecturer and very approachable.

I really enjoyed this class! Professor Engeln is such an engaging and fun professor, so lectures are never boring. The content itself was also more interesting than a lot of other classes I've taken. Most of your grade is determined by three exams, but they're not cumulative and they're only multiple–choice questions. The inQuizatives seemed kind of unnecessary, but it was an okay resource. There's lots of extra credit opportunities in this class, and they do make a difference in your grade, so I would definitely take advantage of them. Overall, really liked this class even though it was pretty surface level psychology.

I loved this course! Professor Engeln is an awesome lecturer, and every class was interesting and engaging. She really made an effort to draw us into the material, and she tried to make it fun and entertaining, while also taking the content seriously and pointing out interesting details you might not have noticed at first glance. She was super clear about course expectations and told us exactly what we needed to know for our exams. The assessments and assignments were super fair, and I actually really enjoyed the readings for this course as well.

I loved this class! Renee was such an amazing professor, she is super engaging and I was never bored during her lectures—I loved coming to class every time! It's also pretty easy to do well in this class because Renee offers lots of extra credit opportunities, and the exams were always very straightforward. The exams require memorizing a lot of information which was sometimes tedious, but once you do the exams are pretty easy. I found the content we covered to be super interesting. This was my first time taking a psych class and it definitely made me want to take more. I HIGHLY recommend taking this class with Renee!

This is a very interesting course, especially if you are interested in being a psych major. I will say it's definitely not an easy A; lots of tedious readings and complex material to memorize. Be prepared to cover every aspect of psychology, including neuroscience and biology. Exams are difficult. But Renee is a great professor who is passionate about the topic and provides extra credit opportunities.

Take this class if you want to become a better student. The first few lessons in this course are fully about how to study and get the best grades possible. There is a lot of extra credit, attendance isn't mandatory, and they pretty much tell you how to study. Just study, especially for the first test and you should get a high B or A.

Psychology is such an interested subject that I recommend all Northwestern students look into. If you put in the work and are motivated to learn, a good grade is completely achievable. I can tell that Professor Engeln truly cares about her students and is interested in the topic she teaches. Would recommend to take this course.

Midterms were surprisingly challenging because although the questions were all multiple choice, they require you to apply everything you learned into difficult scenario questions, sometimes focusing on content that was only briefly discussed in a lecture and nowhere else. Otherwise, the lectures and content was very interesting
The tests were not too bad but often Renee would pull out random information that she offhandedly mentioned in lecture which didn't seem relevant to my learning or understanding of material.
Easy exams but readings took a while
Intro to Psych is a good intro class but it has a lot of readings you need to do
I thought Renee was a phenomenal teacher, and she was a great lecturer and made herself very accessible outside of class. I really enjoyed the class and didn't find it to be fairly difficult

Englee is a great lecturer. Studying for exam is easy as long as you take the time to review the questions. Honestly, it feels like more could be talked about in lecture and less focus could have been put on the book because the chapters are long.

I liked that the Inquizitives were what we used to study, that made learning the class material and studying effective and engaging. Renee is great, so the lectures were usually engaging, they can feel long sometimes but it's so easy to keep up.

This class was tough. It honestly felt like a weed–out class which is sad because it's literally psych 110. The content is mildly interesting and Renee is very energetic, but the exams were HARD. There is extra credit opportunities, but often felt like the tests were so unnecessarily hard, especially compared to other psych 110 professors. Would probably recommend taking this class with a different prof.

This course is a bit more work than you might expect, but its really great and interesting! The textbook chapters are long and do take a while, but they help so much with the exam. If you don't cram and do all the review questions as you go, the class feels really manageable. If you stay on top of things, you can definitely get an A. Go to the lectures, Renee really keeps you engaged and some content she tests on is only covered in class.
The professor is very engaging in lectures and is highly encourages participation.
Renee was the best professor I've ever had. She always made me laugh during her lectures and gave multiple opportunities for extra credit. The course was so interesting and also a lot of fun.
very interesting presentations to get you interested in the course.

Prof. Engeln is the single best professor I have ever had. I went to every lecture purely because it was always fun, and I learned a lot (very easy to get an A if you don't act like a bum). Take this class if you have any reason to even consider it!

Pretty solid intro psych class. There was more about research methods and neuroscience than a typical course I think but it wasn't too bad. The exams are essentially your entire grade, and they are difficult, but you have lots of time to prepare and studying isn't too hard. Definitely answer the lecture and reading review questions as you go because that is great for gradually accumulating info. There are always some really difficult questions each exam and even if you study you can't guarantee an amazing score, but they aren't impossible by any means.
Renee is a great lecturer! Tests are on the harder side but she prepares you well for them.

Renee is an incredible professor and such an engaging lecturer. The content was interesting and I enjoyed learning more about psychology. I will say, that between the lab participation requirement and preparing for the midterms –– the course could be fairly time–consuming and the exams were hyper–specific. You have to set aside time to really study and make sure you know exactly what's going on (and some of the book information isn't covered in class). I'd still recommend the course though if you're genuinely interested in psychology.

I LOVED this class. Renee is an amazing professor, and every lecture was so interesting and fun. If you get the chance, go to her office hours and ask her about her research––It's so cool. You can tell she really cares about her students, beyond just the realm of academic success. I learned a bunch of different things in this class about myself and the world around me that I've already put into practice. It's important to study for the tests, but this is pretty straightforward if you do the review questions Renee provides as they come out.
This is a great foundation of psychology, super interesting, pretty straight–forward in terms of what you need to know

Renee is a very passionate professor. However, I would not recommend taking this class with her unless you are SUPER interested in psychology. The exam questions are very hard and tense. On our first exam, she asked a question about an example she gave in class – this is how specific the questions are. It's definetly not an easy A class...

I LOVED THIS CLASS!! All bow down to Prof. Rene Engeln!! She is one of the best professors at this university, hands down. She is witty, down–to–earth, fun, and above all – an AMAZING teacher!! It's definitely a lot of studying for the exams, I won't lie – but it's so worth it if you're genuinely interested in the subject!
Very good

RENEE ENGELN IS MOTHER. TAKE THIS CLASS WITH PROF ENGELN. she is so enthusiastic about what she teaches and such an engaging, fantastic lecturer. 80 minute classes can feel long but she does a great job of keeping it fun and interesting the whole time. if you enjoy her teaching, you can also go to her office hours to chat with her, ask questions, and hear about topics from students in other psych classes too, which was cool. the class itself is pretty packed with information and the readings can be a little overwhelming, but if you do all the assigned work and go to lecture you should learn a lot and perform well on exams (I am an engineering student and it was totally manageable alongside my other stem classes). the review questions are the most important study material, they tell you exactly what you need to know for the (3, non–cumulative) exams. this was my first psych class ever and I learned so much interesting content, since it provides an intro to many many different areas of psychological study. I'll definitely be taking another psych class with renee in the future, this was a fantastic experience and I highly recommend if you have any interest in psych or people at all.

All this considered, intro psych is very interesting, however, the tests are beyond brutal. Honestly, if you were thinking of taking this class to get a free A without putting in much effort, then think again. If I were to do the course again, I would REALLY do as the professor says and read 1 textbook chapter every week instead of cramming right before the test. So overall, a great class, but very challenging.

It was an okay class. Not an easy A. You definitely need to understand the processes. Although the class is not recorded and she suggests you to go to class if you learn best from a textbook you will learn more on that than the lectures. The lectures were just an add on. Id recommend the class but you do need to put effort in.

Renee is really good at giving lectures. Lectures usually feel more like a TED talk rather than a class. The grading system is also super chill, I never really felt stressed about this class. Most of the content is really interesting, so even when you do have work, it doesn't feel like a drag.

This class was a great intro to psychology, which did a good job of broadly covering as much of the basics as possible in a quarter class. While tests weren't easy, there were plenty of extra credit opportunities that I appreciated and the homework was a great study tool in and of itself. By going to every lecture and engaging yourself by diligently taking notes, you learn a lot in this class
No specific reaction

This class is very interesting. The tests are a bit difficult, and there is a ton of information but definitely a cool class. I would not say an easy A though. Renee is more of an entertainer than a lecturer.

The class is super interesting. The tests are very hard. The best way to do well in this class is to learn the test, learn how to identify the concepts within a multiple choice test. Also, do the EC papers, they are easy and kind of fun. They also help you study.
very interesting topics
It's a lot of memorization and a lot of the questions are ambiguous. If you understand the concepts well you should do fine. The concepts themselves are not very challenging.
This is a great intro course to take. It's low stakes, hardly any homework, and pretty interesting. The tests were kinda hard but as long as you give yourself ample time to prepare, you'll do great.

Professor Engeln was genuinely the best professor I have had at NU, and one of my favorite teachers of all time. Not only is she passionate about the material and a clear instructor, but she is one of the most genuine and nice people you will ever meet. She is always excited to talk in her office hours about any topic, and makes students feel very comfortable both in and out of class. For a huge introductory course, she really made an effort to get to know people in the class, and engage with individuals. The tests were very well designed to test our knowledge, and did not look to trick you... she even made a conscious effort to remove questions that tripped people up, taking the responsibiliy herself for those questions being poorly designed, and not blaming students for being incompetent. I highly recommend this course to anyone looking for a distro or anyone remotely interested in psychology if Professor Engeln is teaching it, you will love it!

The professor had an amazing attitude but the questions on her exams were worded a bit confusingly and your grade is based on 3 exams. You have to study A LOT and its a class that will require you to attend lectures every day. She does give extra credit but you have to study to do well in this class.

Take this class with Engeln. Super fun and engaging professor, I was always interested in what was going on in the lectures. The readings could be tedious and took a fair bit of time but I did feel like i was learning interesting things from it. I loved how Engeln posted review questions for textbook readings and lecture notes to help us prepare for exams, which were not that hard if you prepared well with those review questions. Also a lot of extra credit opportunities which was awesome. Definitely recommend this class with Engeln, great option as a distribution requirement.

Renee is an INCREDIBLE professor, and she is very accessible and easy to come to when you need to discuss something with her. All else aside, this class is a lot of content and really requires you to pay attention in class and do the textbook readings for the exams. GO TO CLASS!!! She discusses things in lecture that aren't covered in the textbook but ARE covered on the exam. Take detailed notes and make quizlets and you will be fine.

Professor was okay. Great speaker, but I am not sure of her instruction. She read off of slides and never recorded her lectures. You had to be in lecture to get all the information because the slides literally had like a word. There were also random assignments spread throughout the quarter that could only completed in class. Exams are okay, just long and weirdly worded. Inquisitives, the homework, are kinda dumb. Not bad course, just meh

Solid distro, with little homework, multiple choice exams which are straightforward most of the time. but you need to study quite a bit because the questions are very specific and you need to understand the material well. the reading is unnecessarily long sometimes but manageable. I would recommend the class solely because I think it changed how I think about myself in a lot of ways and was transformational in that sense. I guarantee you will also learn a bunch of things about yourself.
This class taught me a lot. Professor Renee was a great lecturer and was very helpful and answered questions really well.

This course was very interesting, but it was often hard to stay on top of the work. She didn't check that you read the readings and didn't really reference any of the reading material in class, so it was easy to get behind. The tests were also very difficult and required a lot of memorization, but luckily the material was all very interesting so studying wasn't too bad.

First of all, I wanted to say that Professor Engeln is one of the most engaging professors I've had so far! I was constantly captivated by her lecture style and there was never a moment where I felt bored or uninterested. She explained concepts and ideas so well and connected them to the real world and her own life, which made them so much more authentic and understandable. I will say that you really had to study in this course to do well– there was no way around it. If you study and put in the work, you will be fine, but if you start slacking off or do the review questions at the very last minute, you will struggle. Professor Engeln always made her expectations very clear and explicit and I really liked the way she structured this course.

Engeln was a great professor; she was blunt and straightforward but also clearly passionate about psychology. It was a very test heavy course, but if you studied and prepared sufficiently, over a decent amount of time and preparation, you would be fine. It is not a class that you can skip and do well in. It is extremely participation and attendance dependent, and if you are not at every lecture, it is often that you will miss things on the exams. However, if you attend class, do the readings, and start studying for the exam with sufficient time to prepare, you will be fine. Engeln was a great professor and she motivated her students to come to class and kept everything very entertaining and applicable to real world examples. Definitely do not think this class will be an easy A, but you will learn a lot.

This course is definitely not as easy as you might think. There is a lot of reading and sometimes it gets hard to keep up with everything. A good amount of what you read will not be covered in class and will still be tested on the exam. Use flashcards, and distribute your revision as it gets harder to memorize everything last minute. I'd also highly suggest to do all the extra credits assignments; they might raise your grade. There are also in–class assignments. They are worth less than 3% but it is still a good addition to your grades; so attend lectures. There also subtle things that the professor might say in class that will appear on the exam, so focus when in class, I'd suggest putting your phone away and maybe trying to take your notes in a notebook instead of using an ipad. THIS IS NOT AN EASY DISTRO CLASS! If you are genuinely interested in psych, you will love professor Renee, but if you are just looking for a distro, this will definitely be a challenging class (I found it to be very biology oriented). There is also a lab requirement which can get stressful and very annoying ( lots of surveys to complete).

I LOVED this class. I would definitely recommend taking Intro to Psych with Renee. She is such a good Professor and keeps the students so interested throughout her lectures. The grading in this class is composed of three big multiple choice exams, so if you're not a good test taker, that could be a cause for concern. However, I wouldn't let that deter you from taking the class as the tests are not that bad in terms of difficulty as long as you pay attention in class and look over the review sheets posted.

Do not take this class if you don't need to. I was hoping for a chill class where I could engage with my interest in psychology. In reality, it was WAY too time–consuming for an intro class. It was far too much memorization and you kind of have to go to class...but class doesn't teach you much.

The intro to psych course covers all the basics of psychology and catch us non AP Psych students up to students that already have that advantage, albeit fitting an year's worth of psyhology into a quarter.

I loved this course. Even though it was 300 people, I was super engaged and interesting in the lecture material. Prof. Engeln is a great lecturer; she is fun and nice and also good at her job. I think this class is super manageable if you read the textbook and go to lectures. That's literally all you need to do to succeed; putting in the effort will bring you reward.

The textbook is everything in this class. All the material for the exams is in the textbook and not everything is covered in class. However, there are also some important studies mentioned in class that are on the tests so it's important to attend class. Renee was a good teacher and clearly both knowledgeable and enthusiastic but sometimes went on tangents that made the class turn into a stand–up comedy esk routine.
it was very insightful

This class is really interesting but you definitely have to work for the A. There's a lot of material to get through and the exams come very quickly (although they're not cumulative which is really nice). You need to go to class because the slides aren't usually detailed enough to supplement what you'd miss. STUDY THE WAY YOU'RE TOLD. On the first day, Renee told us that she'd post review questions for the textbook and lectures and to only focus on that content when studying. She also recommended not annotating the textbook because it's a waste of time so keep that in mind when reading.
highly recommend!
Good course, mistake was not studying early, review before and after class and make sure to take notes during lectures because some specific things talked about in class pop up on the exams
Renee is awesome! Great introduction, made me interested in psych, stayed interested during the 80 minute lectures which is rare.
I learned a lot of interesting information and overall enjoyed the course and instruction.
the teacher is amazing but be sure to pay attention to the examples she shows in class they might show up on the exam
We didn't learn much about personalities and clinical psychology, but I loved the teaching style.

This course is not an extremely easy distribution requirement, yet it is still incredibly achievable given you are able to set aside the proper amount of time for it. One thing I wish I would have known going into this course is that your grade is almost entirely composed of exam scores, so if you are a bad test–taker, that is something to keep in mind for this course.

This was an excellent intro to psych course. Professor Engeln was engaging and upbeat and managed to make each class interesting. Your grade in the class is pretty test–centered, but going to class, keeping up with the reading, and completing the review questions almost guarantees a good grade. The lab–participation component is honestly a fun way to get a little more engaged in the course work, just don't push them off till the end of the quarter.

source_file: Student Report for Mary Finch (SOCIOL_110-0_20  Introduction to Sociology)_8183b706-736d-4412-aa28-a6aaaba345caen-US.pdf

likert_questions.question_text: Provide an overall rating of the instruction.
Provide an overall rating of the course.
Estimate how much you learned in the course.
Rate the effectiveness of the course in challenging you intellectually.
Rate the effectiveness of the instructor in stimulating your interest in the subject.

categorical_questions.question_text: Estimate the average number of hours per week you spent on this course outside of class and lab time
What is the name of your school?
Your Class
What is your reason for taking the course?
What was your interest in this subject before taking the course?

comments: This class is good if you have a good TA, because the TA does all of the grading. The professor is good at lecturing and the material is easy to follow if you at least show up to class. It's a very manageable amount of work. However, the discussion section I had was not good and it ruined my experience with this course.

I really enjoyed the second part of the course, when we finished theory and moved onto discussions of race, gender, sexuality, and class. It was really great to be reading from authors who I could relate to.

The lectures were pretty useless but the powerpoints help you understand the context of the readings that you had for the day of that lecture. She showed so many videos that it's basically an RTVF class. What's really important though, is her powerpoints and the articles––the midterm/final is solely based on your ability to memorize definitions and write answers to short/long response questions based on said articles. As long as you can do this, this is an easy A distro. There's also 2 field notes and 2 observational analyses––2 hours for each field notes section and 1000 words per observation analysis. It's pretty easy to do. I enjoyed this class and learned a lot, despite it being a lot of readings and a lot of memorization.
great course. work hard
Field Notes are difficult but midterm was pretty easy.

The exams in this class are very easy; it is easy to score near perfect on both the midterm and final. The other bulk of your grade comes from the observation project. How you do on this project is very reliant on how your TA grades, which is frustrating knowing you might how done better on certain assignments if you had a different TA. While the content of the class was very interesting, the discussion sections seemed a little pointless, and the class tended to get boring towards the end of the hour.
The course was pretty easy
This class is an easy A but it's pretty dry. Would recommend as a distro but otherwise take another sociology class

Professor Finch really loves what she is teaching, and its super clear in class. The lectures are a bit long, but she tries to make them interesting for us and shortens them whenever possible. The exams directly represent what was covered in class and we are given clear outlines on what to prepare for.
This course is really easy, the tests are really straightforward, and the book is not really a necessity as all of the important highlights of the assigned section are gone over in lecture.

This course it very relevant to today and applies the lessons to the issues of today.

4/9

I really enjoyed this class! All of the topics are super interesting and are very relatable to my own experiences in society. Professor Finch is super sweet although her teaching style is a little bit awkward in my opinion. It's not a big deal because her powerpoints are super clear. There's also only one midterm and a final which is extremely nice. She also gives you a study guide for each and her exams follow them to a T which makes studying for them very easy. She even gives you the open ended questions so as long as you fill out the guide you'll be fine. There's also two observation papers that are not bad at all and only ended up being around 3 pages each. I personally felt like the discussions were unnecessary because all of the topics were so straightforward. I know the previous quarters didn't have discussion and just had another paper which I personally would have preferred.

This course was about as bland as they come. The instruction came directly out of the powerpoints and we could have easily learned things without attending lecture. The exams and essays are easy, but I felt like I was cheated in content. The reading assignments are cruel in length, and it is very easy to fall behind if you aren't careful. Professor Finch is very distant and detached from her students, and it felt like a chore listening to her talk because she seemed to lack passion for the subject. We watched way too many short videos and then were tested on the content of the videos without formal discussion of them. I did not enjoy this class, but if it means going up to higher level sociology course then you have to do what you have to do...

Being an intro class, it just gives a broad overview of a lot of topics, especially race and class issues. Professor Finch is very passionate about social justice, and is available to help students if needed. There is one midterm, one final, and two observation field notes/analysis assignments. Overall easy class.
This class was interesting but the readings were a bit much.
Was a really good distro. Clear material and the exams were very fair and followed the study guides they provided.
Very interesting class. It's very easy to do well.
if you are a mccormick student looking for an easy theme elective to take, this is the course for you!! a very easy A if you have common knowledge of topics like race, class, gender, etc
I felt very woke because of this class. Would recommend.
Great course if interested in sociology and doesn’t take substantial effort to obtain a good grade.
Some of the topics were kind of self–explanatory but there are a number of topics in the course that is interesting to learn.

This class is definitely a nice GPA booster if you need to fill a distro. If you are interested in sociology, I do recommend taking this class. However, don't rely on the lectures to get the most out of the class...Prof. Finch is very sweet and clearly passionate about sociology, but her slides are copied and pasted from the textbook then further read verbatim. However, if you want to get the most out of the class, keep up with the weekly readings (they're actually very interesting and will help you understand the course concepts!!)
I thought the course was really easy. Not challenged at all.

This course is 80% leftist propaganda. The way the course is structured does not encourage critical thinking but rather requires unquestioning acceptance of leftist ideology. If you are a conservative or moderate, you will be thinking of 10 well–reasoned objections to many of points made. I was really hoping to learn sociological theory and the ideas that make up the basis of the field rather than hot–button radical leftist crusades. I wish less time was spent on the professor's political views and more time on sociology.

I took this course because I wanted to learn more about our society and broaden my perspective regarding various issues extant within the world at large, and I'm happy to say that it was successful in helping me achieve those goals. While not extensively in– depth, this course provides a good introduction to the field of sociology, and has definitely piqued my interest in the subject thanks to its engaging lectures and unique, interesting assignments.

This class is a decent distro; it's not very hard. I thought the amount of information covered was good, but lecture was mostly just reading off the slides. The readings get to be a little much at times; I would say do the readings for the midterm and then decide if you want to do them or not after that. Other than reading, homework workload is light; just 2 observations and 2 3–page papers. This class does NOT need a discussion section, though the TAs are nice. Also, I completely hated the textbook because it couldn't go 5 pages without bragging about how sociology is better than all of the other social sciences. Midterm was incredibly fair; you get study guides for the exams, which are a godsend.
This course was great. Professor Finch is a great lecturer and the material is very engaging.
Lectures were ok but at times were very boring.
The class was not very difficult yet it was still interesting to learn about the concepts. The class is definitely less intensive than most classes which makes the pace feel rather relaxed.
super easy course, very boring if you’re a minority or come from a diverse place. The study guide is the exam.

It was a pretty easy intro class, and the material is actually really interesting. I would advise everyone to take an intro to soc class and Professor Finch explained things clearly. I also liked having a discussion section.
Overall I liked the class, but the discussion section was so extra. I'd rather have another paper then go to a useless 50 minute discussion session

I'm a big fan of professor Mary Finch. Her readings are relatively interesting compared to my other classes, her lecture style and

5/9 examples she gave in class can be interesting a lot of times, plus I was interested in sociology the first place. She would give you study guides before exams, and her exams are reasonably hard. If you study well, you would be fine.
This class was really eye opening on a lot of topics, but did not push my critical understanding of sociological topics with any regularity.
Readings were sometimes unnecessarily long and lectures got kind of repetitive. But exams are easy as long as study guide is used.

This class was kinda fun. The participant observation assignment was very interesting and I had a lot of fun doing it. I would stress that you HAVE to do all of the reading because there are tons of things to read each week and they're all super long
The course was stimulating and engaging. The material is important to learn and understand

This class is pretty good and not super intense. The readings are good but often really long, but you don't technically need to do them until you're tested on them. I think that the midterm was really fair. The final felt rushed due to the short and medium answer questions needing a lot of information, but was fair to what we knew. You will be able to prepare well because she gives you a study sheet with all the terms and all of the essay questions. The observation papers were pretty chill. Overall, a pretty good class with not a crazy amount of work and fair assessments.
This course was fine and didn't take too much of my time and Mary Beth Finch is a fine teacher if you actually go to lecture.
This class is very useful since I have never taken a sociology class before. I was able to learn the terms and everything necessary for the MCAT.
Great distro, really interesting, and TAs are great!

This class is a pretty solid distro since it doesn't have that much of a time commitment. I will admit though that the course felt pretty boring. The topic were very interesting but most of the things we learned weren't really new to me. Everything was so surface level, I genuinely felt that just watching a few documentaries and reading some articles on my free time would have put me at the same spot as this course had. Regardless, I do hope there were a few people in the class that learned something new and interesting from it. The discussion of race and poverty is important and even if this class didn't really introduce me to anything new, I hope it did for other people.
ability to analyze pieces of life from the perspective of sociology.
The lectures were pretty helpful in explaining the material we read about. The professor is really engaged as well. The class was fairly interesting.
I really enjoyed this class & as an engineer it was a much needed change up from my other classes.

This was a good introductory course. The readings were very clear about the topics they covered and helped promote understanding of the things discussed in class. The exams focused on the key concepts discussed in class.

For an introductory sociology course, I felt pretty satisfied with what I learned. The concepts are interesting and not difficult to grasp as long as you pay attention to the lectures/do the readings, and Prof. Finch was always willing to offer clarifications for anything that was (she usually did so based on our weekly reflections, which are much shorter than they sound). That being said, I think I would've done just as well grade–wise without attending lecture, which might be of concern to some but a blessing to others. I did not find the course challenging or time–consuming at all, but again considering that it's an intro class, I suppose that sounds right. Would recommend to anyone who wants to learn more about prominent social issues without the commitment of an advanced class.
This is a very good course to take if you want to take a class that has some connection to "real life." The content is fairly intuitive except for specific vocabulary. Just know the definitions.
It is a pretty good course. Final was pretty difficult tho

This is a good intro to sociology course as well as an easy but interesting distro. The readings are long and time consuming, however, they are very intriguing and you can get a lot from it if you actually take the time to read. I really enjoyed the videos that were played during lectures. The study guide is very similar to the midterm and final so as long as you complete them you should do great in the course. We also had to do two sets of field notes and analysis essays, but they're very easy to do. The TA in my discussion section did a great job at going over the material and making sure we could apply it to things we see in today's society.

I really enjoyed this class, even though I wasn't super interested before taking it. I think everyone should have to take this class regardless of major, because you really learn a lot about society and privilege, as well as learn about experiences that you may have the privilege of not experiencing. This really helps develop empathy and understanding. The coursework wasn't too bad at all. The exams covered material from lecture and the readings, so it is important to at least skim the readings. The observing project seemed hard at first, but it was actually really interesting and easy once you utilized the concepts in class and talked with TAs.
Most concepts are taught through the textbook. Lectures are basically the professor showing various videos that I didn’t find as helpful.

This course is very interesting and you can learn a lot about the basics of sociological concepts even if you have been exposed to sociology before. Concepts are well–illustrated through a variety of examples and the workload is very reasonable.

I enjoyed this class because it relates to many concurrent issues/events going on. Overall, this class was very interesting for an

6/9 introductory course and the workload was not bad at all.

This course is pretty straightforward. There are two papers, a midterm, and a final that make up your grade. The papers are pretty short, and the exams are based on a study guide that is given well in advance. This is a pretty easy distro, but the course material is also interesting.
easy, not interesting

This course was very well designed for an introductory class. It cover different topics, but I feel like it gets to the big ideas of each which is good! I would recommend taking it with Prof. Finch because her lectures are so easy to follow and examples are useful. Her study guides are useful too!

7/9
