comments: Professor Ratliff is so nice and a great lecturer. The exams were very chill, and we only had one online homework assignment per week plus prelecture videos to watch. I really enjoyed the class not having any prior experience in physics.

I appreciated that Prof. Ratliff made the class very manageable in terms of the weekly workload and the examinations. It was nice to get finished before finals week. You can tell she cares about teaching students!

If you have to take physics, you should 1000% take it with Ratliff. She really cares about her students and is also a good teacher (she explains all the concepts really well). She also teaches the class in a pretty interesting and engaging way. For this quarter, there were group exams (and no final), which was really helpful. I was initially worried about not knowing enough calculus, but I was totally fine and any new concepts (like dot product) are clearly explained.
This is a wonderful course and really well structured. Professor Ratliff is a great lecturer!
Stress free physics due to lenient policies and group exams.

Prof Ratliff is amazing!!! Not just saying this because it’s easy to do well on her class despite the difficulty of the content but seriously she actually CARES about her students and is so passionate about physics. She is such a competent professor who makes learning in this class very enjoyable. Best STEM class at NU hands down .

Coming into this course, I hadn't taken physics in several years and had no real interest in it. However, Professor Ratliff was able to explain concepts in a way that was understandable and then reinforced those concepts with a white board assignment each class. There is a lot of busy work for this course, including pre–lectures, white board assignments, and homework, but I think it was essential in learning the concepts; also, Ratliff is flexible with extensions if needed! There are 3 exams (no final), which were fairly made and graded as well!

Amazing course with Professor Ratliff. She communicated the physics concepts clearly and was incredibly approachable as a professor. She stresses that her students truly learn rather than stress over exams so her exams were taken in groups and were online. This might change in the future but it was a great system and took the pressure off the difficulty of physics.
Just a great class, Ratliffe is your best option for this class, helps to have taken physics in high school’s
Professor Ratllif made a Physics intro class so interesting! Amazing person and professional.

Pre–lectures are a great resource, class solidifies it, homework is good practice, exams are a little iffy (Ratliff listens to what the class wants and structures appropriately so it only makes sense that group exams are harder). Professor Ratliff was always ready to help and could answer any questions
Take it with Ratliff, she’s the kindest stem prof I have ever had and makes the content super interesting and engaging.

This is a good course. The professor is very kind and always willing to help and support her students. I was not a big fan of the lectures because I found them to not be very interesting. I wish all of the material and problems were done on the professor's digital device because it was really hard to read the notes she would write on the chalkboard. Overall, it was a great class. The HW was hard sometimes, but you are given a good amount of time to do it and get help if needed. The exams were fair.
love Ratliff, she is genuinely interested in teaching physics
TAKE THIS WITH PROF RATLIFF!! She is literally the best and makes physics possible to learn (coming from someone who has never taken physics before)!!
Prof. Ratliff is just too nice. The exams are group–based, which makes life so much easier. I learned a lot as well as feeling very rewarding. Def take 135 with Ratliff if you can!!

Prof. Ratliff is amazing. She wants the best for all of her students and listens to/seeks our feedback constantly. Three group exams, no final. The material was very challenging at times and Prof. Ratliff was always willing to help us fully understand during office hours.
This is a very easy class. Please take with Prof Ratliff she is super nice and very enthusiastic about teaching and helping you.

this class w ratliff was great. she cares about her students and their mental health more so than any other stem prof i have encountered. sometimes her lectures were rambly, making them hard to follow. i LOVE the structure of her class and it made it so easy to learn without feeling the constant pressure of grades
I am not a physics person at all and it often confuses me, however Ratliff really helped us learn the fundamentals and encouraged collaboration so we could use each other as resources.
Professor Ratliff is amazing! If you have to take physics you MUST take it with her –– she is an amazing and kind professor.

I loved Professor Ratliffe! Despite not being great at math, I really felt as though she helped me learn in 135–1 and she was the sweetest and most caring STEM professor I've had. She is very flexible if you're struggling with homework and is willing to explain things to you if you need help during office hours. She's really great, make sure to take this course with her!!

10/10 recommend this class and this professor! She is amazing and really cares about her students. I liked how collaborative this class was and enjoyed taking it. The pre–lecture videos are very boring, but I love how we do whiteboard assignments in class and she welcomes questions with open arms.

Professor Ratliff is very nice, almost too nice to the point that I don't feel like I'm incentivized to learn in her class. At the beginning of the quarter, I was staying up to data with lecture and homework assignments. However, as work starting building up in other classes, I really lost the motivation to keep attending class, since the exams were worked on in groups and could be submitted at 8pm the night of.

Prof. Ratliff is the absolute best! She cares so much about her students, and the accommodations she provides compared to a traditional course format (e.g. group exams) absolutely helped us learn in a less stressed environment. We could focus on understanding the underlying concepts without wasting time stressing over memorization. I was scared to take physics at NU but Prof. Ratliff made it very approachable!

I felt at ease with the pacing of the course. I often find that STEM courses at Northwestern can be very overwhelming because of how fast they go. However, with the prelectures and the slower pacing of physics, I did not encounter any issues.

Professor Ratliff is a great professor and provides tons of resources to help you understand the material and do well on the exams. If you do the homework, whiteboards, and practice problems you'll be well prepared.
Please take if you have the option to with Ratliffe! She is genuinely so amazing and such a great professor as well!
Ratliff is so kind and her class is very chill. Just do the work and it's not hard.
Professor Ratliff is the best!! She actually cares about students' mental health, which is huge in the pre–med world. Definitely take this class with her!

Professor Ratliff is very nice, possibly one of the nicest professors. But the class was very extremely easy, maybe too easy. Achieve was helpful. It's a class that you get what you put in for sure. Professor Ratliff clearly cares a lot for her students. She is a great lecturer that has very engaging lecture examples if you choose to be engaged.

This course was amazing. A wonderful introduction to Physics. Professor Ratliff is nothing but the best. She is so passionate about what she teaches, and truly makes Physics so fun and easy to understand.

The course was really well setup. Professor R is a very good instructor that cares about her students. It was really helpful that some of the whiteboard assignments at the end of class were homework problems which provides more opportunity to use time wisely. The group exams were very beneficial because it relieved stress and allowed students to work together and do better on exams.

Professor Ratliff is a great professor. She teaches really well and is very approachable to ask questions. Also always willing to find ways to improve her course. The content itself is fairly easy, the first third is a review of 230–1 and then you go into mechanics. Overall simple physics course not hard
Prof. Ratliff is amazing. she is so helpful and kind and intelligent

I loved Ratliff. She is incredibly understanding and helpful. She made physics fun and her enthusiasm is contagious. However, I do wish we spent more time and content and less on the whiteboard. Because lecture was shorter, there were a couple of times where I did not know what to do for the whiteboard. This would stress me out. However, she makes her class heavier on participation, which I appreciated. The group exams were incredibly beneficial for me. They made me think is ways I did not did before. They were challenging and would push us to think. Further, there could be a problem similar to a whiteboard question, but then she would change something. Then I would have to think outside of the box. There were times I was definitely stumped and my partners helped me.

Take this course! Prof Ratliff is the best and an amazing professor. She's always willing to help and truly wants all of her students to walk away with an enhanced understanding of physics/mechanics. Even if you are not 100% confident in your math/physics skills, trust that Prof Ratliff is such a skilled professor that she will ease you into the concepts.

Prof Ratliff was very good at explaining concepts and showing examples. I appreciate how often assignments, whiteboard assignments, discussions, and labs kept me regularly engaged with the subject. If your can, absolutely take this class with Ratliff
Professor Ratliff is amazing! I hate physics and she made this class as enjoyable as she possibly could for a physics class. Very nice and great at teaching.
this class is an easy A for STEM people who have struggled through bio and chem. The exams are group exams so you take them with an assigned group and can help each other outside of class.

Take this course with Ratliff! She is the sweetest professor and cares about her students doing well! Truly one of the best STEM professors at this school and she does her best to lower the amount of pressure placed on her students.

Professor Ratliff is the best. She is so sweet and understanding and highly considers student opinions when structuring her course. The exams were all collaborative group tests so you are basically guaranteed to do well. As long as you keep up with the homeworks and daily whiteboard assignments the class is pretty low demand.

Professor Ratliff is genuinely the kindest, most incredible professor I've ever had. She truly cares about her students and their learning, something that I feel like is very hard to see in a lot of other professors (especially in the STEM environment). She understands that we are human and does not try to give us ridiculously hard or unreasonable exams that I feel don't test our knowledge of the subject/our learning in the class well. Physics is hard no matter who you take the course with, but Professor Ratliff genuinely made me enjoy it and look forward to her lecture because of her true passion and enthusiasm for the content, which was beyond inspiring. I could probably ramble on for SO long saying how great she is and how much I recommend taking the course with her.
Ratliff is an amazing professor!
